learningGoals: Identify and name 3 basic parts of a wetland plant.
Understand that wetlands absorb water like a sponge.
Use 'I see...' and 'It is...' to describe colors and textures.
lessons.activitySummary: Students will go on a sensory scavenger hunt to touch mud and leaves, then perform a 'Sponge Experiment' to see how wetlands prevent flooding by absorbing water. They will end by sketching their favorite plant in a field journal.
lessons.safetyNotes: Stay on the wooden boardwalks; do not touch unknown berries or flowers.

lessons.location: East Lake Greenway (Liyuan Entrance), Wuchang District
learningGoals: Use binoculars to spot and count 3 different types of birds.
Compare different bird beaks and explain what they eat.
Practice using action verbs like 'fly', 'swim', and 'eat'.
lessons.activitySummary: Students will use binoculars to observe migratory birds on East Lake and use a 'Beak Tool Kit' (tweezers, spoons, clips) to simulate how different birds catch food in the water. They will record their findings in a tally chart.
lessons.safetyNotes: Keep a safe distance from the water edge; watch out for bicycles on the Greenway.

learningGoals: Find and describe insects living in the tall grass and mud.
Count the number of legs and body parts on a bug.
Use prepositions 'on', 'under', and 'in' to describe bug locations.
lessons.activitySummary: Using magnifying glasses, students will explore the riverbank marsh to find 'mini-beasts'. They will build a temporary 'Bug Hotel' using sticks and leaves to understand insect habitats before releasing the bugs.
lessons.safetyNotes: Wear long pants to avoid mosquito bites; stay away from the deep river current.

learningGoals: Demonstrate how sand and stones can make dirty water look clear.
Understand why humans must protect clean water for animals.
Use 'First', 'Next', and 'Last' to describe a process.
lessons.activitySummary: Students will collect lake water and build a natural filtration tower using layers of pebbles, sand, and charcoal. They will observe the change in water clarity and discuss how to keep Wuhan's lakes clean.
lessons.safetyNotes: Do not drink the lake water or the filtered water; wash hands thoroughly after the activity.
